figures.caption: Fig. 3. Performance in the ablation study of the proposed model.
Fig. 2. Detailed performance of the proposed model.

Fig. 1. Overview of the Orthogonal Task Decomposition (OrthTD) framework. The figure is composed of two parts. Part 1 (Framework Overview) illustrates how heterogeneous multimodal clinical data (e.g., tabular variables, vital signs, and clinical text) are fused into a unified patient representation, which is then decomposed into a shared component capturing general risk and task-specific components encoding outcome-dependent risk, enabling structured multi-task learning. Part 2 (Model Architecture) details the implementation: modality-specific encoders project inputs into a common latent space, which is fused by a Transformer with a learnable global token to form a multimodal representation. This representation is then decomposed into shared and task-specific subspaces for each outcome, with an orthogonality regularization enforcing non-redundancy between them, and task-specific prediction heads produce probabilistic estimates for multiple clinical outcomes.
